entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in southern Mali

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in southern Mali

entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in southern Mali

entities.name: SOUTHERN MALI
entities.description: The geographic region in Mali where the study was conducted

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area

entities.name: YIELD VARIABILITY
entities.description: The variation in crop yield over time or space

entities.name: RISK MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of quantifying and mitigating production hazards to inform decision making

entities.name: LATE ONSET OF RAINS
entities.description: A weather hazard where rains start later than usual

entities.name: INSUFFICIENT TOTAL RAINFALL
entities.description: A weather hazard characterized by low overall precipitation

entities.name: DRY SPELLS
entities.description: Periods of time with little or no rainfall during the growing season

entities.name: LOW FERTILISER QUALITY
entities.description: A non-weather hazard involving poor quality of input fertilizers

entities.name: SUDDEN LACK OF LABOUR
entities.description: A non-weather hazard involving the unavailability of workforce

entities.name: DSSAT CROP MODEL
entities.description: A software tool used to simulate crop growth and yield under various conditions

entities.name: RISK MATRIX
entities.description: A tool used to visualize the frequency and impact of hazards

entities.name: N APPLICATION
entities.description: The process of applying nitrogen fertilizer to crops

entities.name: EARLY SOWING
entities.description: The practice of planting seeds earlier than the baseline date

entities.name: SHORT DURATION VARIETIES
entities.description: Crop varieties that reach maturity in a shorter time

entities.name: LONG DURATION VARIETIES
entities.description: Crop varieties that take longer to reach maturity

entities.name: FARM DIVERSITY
entities.description: The practice of growing multiple crop types to spread risk

entities.name: OPERATIONAL FARM FLEXIBILITY
entities.description: The ability of a farm to adapt management practices to respond to risks
